details: :edit {file} opens a file for editing
Write first or use :edit! if you want to discard current changes
:find searches Vim's 'path' and edits the match
Useful when the file is not in the current directory
:browse opens the file-open dialog
Use it when you want to pick a file instead of typing its name
:buffers, :files, and :ls list known files/buffers
Use the list before switching with :buffer, :bn, :bp, or Ctrl-^

tips.summary: Jump to a listed buffer with :buffer {N}
details: :buffer {N} jumps to the numbered buffer from the buffer list
Use it after :ls when you know exactly which file you want

tips.summary: Step through the argument list with :next / :previous
details: :next moves to the next file in the argument list
:previous moves back to the earlier one

tips.summary: Jump to a specific argument with :argument {N}
details: :argument {N} edits the Nth file in the argument list
Use it when you know exactly which argument-list file you want

tips.summary: Jump to the ends of the argument list with :first / :last
details: :first edits the first file in the argument list
:last edits the last file in the argument list
Jumps to the alternate file
Great for back-and-forth edits
Ctrl-^ is Ctrl-6 on many keyboards
:bn goes to the next buffer
:bp goes to the previous buffer
:bd closes the current buffer/editor tab
Use it when you are done with the file
:e reloads if no changes
:e! discards changes and reloads
:w writes the file
In IdeaVim, this maps to IDE save

tips.summary: Write a copy to another file :w {file}
details: :w {file} writes the current buffer to a different file
Useful when you want a copy without changing the current buffer name
:wa writes all modified buffers
In IDE, typically saves all open editors
:wnext writes the current file and moves to the next one
:wprevious writes and moves to the previous file

tips.summary: Read a file into the current buffer with :read {file}
details: :read {file} inserts another file below the current line
Useful when you want to pull file contents into the current buffer
:q closes if no unsaved changes
:q! forces close/discard (if allowed)
:wq writes and quits
:x writes only if changed
ZZ also writes and quits the current file
:qa exits unless there are changed buffers
:qall closes all editors
Use the force form only if you really want to discard pending changes
:wqa writes changed buffers and exits
:xa is the equivalent all-files write-and-exit command

tips.summary: Home-row movement h j k l
details: h left, j down, k up, l right
Use counts like 10j to jump

tips.summary: Use counts before motions and operators
details: Put a number before a motion or operator
5j moves down 5 lines
3dw deletes 3 words
G goes to the last line
{count}G goes to that line number
Example: 120G jumps to line 120
w next word start
b previous word start
e moves to end of word
ge goes to end of previous word
Nice for edits before punctuation

tips.summary: Big WORD moves W / B / E
details: WORDs split on whitespace
Great for paths and long tokens
gE goes to end of previous WORD
Useful on URLs / file paths
0 start of line
^ first non-blank
$ end of line
g_ jumps to last non-space character
Handy near );}] and trailing spaces

tips.summary: Move to first text on next/prev line + / -
details: + moves down to first non-blank
- moves up to first non-blank
These are motions; use with operators (e.g., d+)
'[ jumps to the first line of the last changed or yanked text
'] jumps to the last line of that text
`[ jumps to the exact start of the last changed or yanked text
`] jumps to the exact end of that text
80| jumps to column 80
Great for line-length edits
gg goes to first line
G goes to last line
Use a count before % to jump by file percentage
50% jumps to middle of file
10% jumps near the top
Bare % jumps to a matching pair instead

tips.summary: Jump to top/middle/bottom of screen H / M / L
details: H top of screen, M middle, L bottom
Great for fast scanning

tips.summary: Move by screen lines on wraps gj / gk
details: gj and gk follow wrapped screen lines
Use them when one long line wraps

tips.summary: Screen-line start/end on wraps g0 / g^ / g$
details: g0 goes to screen-line start
g^ to first text, g$ to screen-line end
{ goes to previous paragraph
} goes to next paragraph
Moves across blank-line-separated blocks
( moves to the previous sentence start
) moves to the next sentence start
Useful in prose, comments, and doc blocks
% jumps between (), {}, and [] by default
Use a count like 50% to jump by file percentage instead
Use to inspect a whole block quickly
`` returns to the exact previous spot
'' returns to the previous line
Useful after big jumps like G, %, /, or file switches
Ctrl-o goes back through older jump locations
Ctrl-i goes forward again
Useful after searches, definitions, and other big jumps
`. jumps to the exact last change
'. jumps to the line of the last change
Useful when you want to get back to where you were editing

tips.summary: Find a character forward on the line f{c}
details: f; jumps to next ';' on the line
Great for long parameter lists

tips.summary: Find a character backward on the line F{c}
details: F; jumps to previous ';' on the line
Use to move precisely without searching

tips.summary: Move till before a character t{c}
details: t) stops just before ')'
Perfect for ct) edits

tips.summary: Move backward till after a character T{c}
details: T) stops just after ')'
Good for edits near opening brackets
; repeats in same direction
, repeats in opposite direction

tips.summary: Place line top/center/bottom zt zz zb
details: zt top, zz center, zb bottom
Use after searches and big jumps

tips.summary: Place first text z<CR> z. z-
details: z<CR> top, z. center, z- bottom
Moves to first non-blank on that line

tips.summary: Page then place first text z+ / z^
details: z+ uses next page, z^ previous page
Then lands at first non-blank
Ctrl-d scrolls down half a page
Ctrl-u scrolls up half a page
Ctrl-f scrolls forward one page
Ctrl-b scrolls back one page
Ctrl-e scrolls down one line
Ctrl-y scrolls up one line

tips.summary: Scroll sideways by columns zh / zl
details: zh scrolls left, zl scrolls right
Needs wrap off or soft-wrap off

tips.summary: Scroll sideways half screen zH / zL
details: zH and zL scroll half a screen
Needs wrap off or soft-wrap off

tips.summary: Place cursor screen edge zs / ze
details: zs shows cursor at screen start
ze shows cursor at screen end
Needs wrap off or soft-wrap off
:set so=5 keeps context lines
Use sidescrolloff for side context
:set scroll=15 changes step size
Useful when half-page feels too big
:set number shows line numbers
:set relativenumber helps with counts
:set list shows tabs and trailing spaces
:set nolist hides them again
:set wrap makes long lines wrap on screen
IDE soft-wrap settings can affect this too
:set incsearch shows matches while typing
Makes /pattern navigation feel instant
:set hlsearch keeps matches highlighted
Clear temporarily with :noh
:set ignorecase smartcase
Uppercase in pattern forces case-sensitive
:set wrapscan continues from top
:set nowrapscan stops at the end
:set clipboard+=unnamedplus
Yank and put use the system clipboard by default
:set clipboard+=ideaput
Uses IntelliJ paste for put commands
In :, :set number? shows the value
Useful when debugging behavior
In :, :set invnumber toggles it
Quick way to flip a boolean option
Esc leaves Insert mode
Ctrl-[ is the same command
i inserts before cursor
a inserts after cursor
In Insert mode, Ctrl-n picks the next word match
Ctrl-p picks the previous one from words already in the file

tips.summary: Insert at line ends I / A
details: I inserts at first non-blank
A appends at end of line

tips.summary: Insert at true line start gI
details: gI inserts at column 1
Unlike I, it keeps indentation
diw deletes just the word
daw deletes the word and trailing space
Use i for inside and a for around
ciw changes just the word, even mid-word
caw changes the word and trailing space
Use i for inside and a for around
ci" changes the text inside double quotes
You can trigger it from anywhere inside the string

tips.summary: Change quotes and content with ca"
details: ca" changes the text and the surrounding double quotes
Useful when you want to replace the whole quoted string

tips.summary: Delete quotes and content with da"
details: da" deletes the quoted text and the surrounding double quotes
Useful when you want to remove the whole string

tips.summary: Change inside parentheses with ci( / cib
details: ci( changes the text inside parentheses
cib is an alias for the same text object

tips.summary: Delete inside or around pairs di( / da(
details: di( deletes the content and keeps the parentheses
da( deletes the content and the surrounding pair
yiw copies just the word
yaw copies the word and trailing space
Use i for inside and a for around
yy yanks the current line
5yy yanks five lines
y0 yanks to start of line
y$ yanks to end of line
p puts after the cursor, or below for linewise text
P puts before the cursor, or above for linewise text
x deletes the character under the cursor and p puts it after
Useful for fixing adjacent typos quickly

tips.summary: Paste and leave cursor after gp / gP
details: gp pastes and moves to end of the pasted text
gP does the same for paste-before
"+y yanks to the OS clipboard
"+p pastes from the OS clipboard
In IdeaVim this depends on IDE clipboard support
"0 keeps the most recent yank
Paste it with "0p even after a delete changes the unnamed register
"1 usually holds the most recent delete
Paste it with "1p after an accidental dd

tips.summary: Store and append text in named registers "a / "A
details: "ayiw stores text in register a; "ap pastes it later
"A appends more text instead of replacing the register
"_d deletes into the black-hole register
Useful when you want to keep the current yank for later pasting

tips.summary: Paste over a selection without losing your yank
details: Select text, then use "_dP
Useful when replacing repeated matches with the same yanked text
:reg shows registers and recorded macros
Use it when you forget what you yanked or recorded
]p pastes after and reindents
[p pastes before and reindents
:m+1 moves the current line down; :m-2 moves it up
:m0 moves it to the top, :m$ to the end, :m2 after line 2
:t. copies the current line below itself
Useful when you want a quick duplicate without yanking
:1,5t. copies lines 1–5 below the cursor
Use 0, $, line numbers, or Visual ranges as the destination

tips.summary: Run a Normal command on many lines with :normal
details: :%normal A; appends ; to every line
Try it on a small range first to avoid accidental bulk edits

tips.summary: Run a substitute only on matching lines with :g
details: :g/ERROR/s/foo/bar/g runs the substitute only on lines matching ERROR
Useful for targeted bulk edits without touching every line
>> shifts the current line right
<< shifts it left; counts work too
>ip shifts the current paragraph or block right
Use other motions too, such as >} or <ap
== recalculates indent for the current line
Use it when manual shifting is not enough
=ip recalculates indent for a paragraph or block
Useful after paste or structural edits
gqap reformats the current paragraph
Use it for wrapped prose or comments; textwidth affects the result
:set textwidth=80 wraps text at 80 columns
gq uses textwidth when formatting prose and comments
o opens below and enters Insert
O opens above and enters Insert

tips.summary: Return to last insert spot gi
details: gi returns to last insert spot
Then it enters Insert again

tips.summary: One Normal command in Insert Ctrl-o
details: Run one Normal command
Then return to Insert

tips.summary: Delete previous word in Insert Ctrl-w
details: Deletes word left of cursor
Good for quick typo fixes

tips.summary: Delete back to indent in Insert Ctrl-u
details: Deletes back to first non-blank
Keeps text after the cursor

tips.summary: Shift indent in Insert Ctrl-t / Ctrl-d
details: Ctrl-t shifts indent right
Ctrl-d shifts indent left

tips.summary: Insert a register in Insert Ctrl-r
details: Ctrl-r 0 inserts last yank
Ctrl-r + inserts clipboard
Type to overwrite text
Esc leaves Replace mode
Ctrl-a repeats last inserted text
Ctrl-@ repeats it and exits Insert
Ctrl-y inserts char from above
Ctrl-e inserts char from below
d{motion} deletes text
c{motion} changes text

tips.summary: Delete/change to line start d0 / c0
details: d0 deletes to line start
c0 changes to line start

tips.summary: Delete/change to first non-blank d^ / c^
details: d^ deletes to first non-blank
c^ changes to first non-blank

tips.summary: Delete/change to line end d$ / c$
details: d$ deletes to line end
c$ changes to line end

tips.summary: Shortcuts to line end D / C
details: D is d$ to line end
C is c$ to line end
x deletes under cursor
X deletes before cursor
dd deletes current line
3dd deletes three lines
s changes one char and inserts
S changes whole line and inserts
cc clears the line
Keeps indent and enters Insert
r replaces the char under cursor
Stays in Normal mode
cw changes to word end
dw deletes to next word start
de deletes to current word end
Useful when dw goes too far
J joins with one space
gJ joins without a space
~ toggles case and moves on
Counts work: 5~

tips.summary: Change case over motion gu / gU
details: gUw uppercases a word
guap lowercases a paragraph
g~iw toggles current word
Useful for style fixes
Ctrl-a increments a number
Ctrl-x decrements a number
Dot repeats the last text change
Do one clean change, then repeat
u undoes the last change
Ctrl-r redoes the undone change
:undo undoes one change
:redo reapplies the undone change

tips.summary: Start a new undo step in Insert Ctrl-g u
details: Starts a new undo step in Insert
Useful after punctuation or pauses
qa records into register a
Press q again to stop
qA appends to macro a
Use when one more step is needed
@a plays macro a
@@ repeats the last macro
10@a runs macro a ten times
Use counts to scale the playback

tips.summary: Run a macro on a range with :normal @r
details: :normal @r runs macro r on a range
Useful for line-by-line cleanup
@: reruns the last : command
Useful after :s, :g, and :normal

tips.summary: Enter Visual mode v / V / Ctrl-v
details: v selects characters
V selects whole lines
Ctrl-v selects a block

tips.summary: Swap selection ends in Visual o
details: o moves cursor to the other end
Great for precise adjustments

tips.summary: Swap block corner in Visual O
details: In block Visual, O swaps the corner
Useful for column edits
`[ jumps to the start of the last change
v starts Visual mode and `] reaches the end

tips.summary: Drop a mark before exploring with ma
details: ma sets a mark before you jump elsewhere
`a returns to the exact position later
`a jumps to the exact marked position
'a jumps to the marked line

tips.summary: Jump to definition or declaration with gd / gD
details: gd jumps to definition through the IDE
gD jumps to declaration when the IDE provides one
gv reselects the last selection
Also reselects text just put with p/P

tips.summary: Select next/prev search match gn / gN
details: gn selects the next search match
gN selects the previous one
Select lines in Visual mode, then run :sort
Use :sort u to keep only unique lines
Select lines in Visual mode, then run :sort n
Use :sort! n for descending numeric order

tips.summary: Select inside a word or paragraph viw / vip
details: viw selects inside the current word
vip selects inside the current paragraph; vap includes surrounding blank lines
Use i for inside and a for around with other text objects too
gg goes to the top, V starts linewise Visual
G extends the selection to the end of the file
> indents, < outdents
Then press . to repeat
>gv reselects after indenting
<gv reselects after outdenting
Select then gu to lowercase
Select then gU to uppercase
Select text then press gq
Great for reflowing comments
Select a column, then press I
Esc applies it to all lines
Select a column, then press A
Esc applies it to all lines
Select a block, then press c
Esc applies it to all lines

tips.summary: Block replace with one char Ctrl-v then r
details: Select a block, then press r
Replaces all selected chars
Type : to get '<,'> range
Use :s, :sort, or :normal
In Visual mode, p replaces the selection with put text
Use "_dP when you want to keep the current yank

tips.summary: Move on command line Ctrl-b / Ctrl-e
details: Ctrl-b jumps to the start
Ctrl-e jumps to the end

tips.summary: Move by word on command line S-Left / S-Right
details: S-Left moves to the previous word
S-Right moves to the next word
Ctrl-c cancels the prompt
Esc cancels it too
In : prompt: Ctrl-w deletes a word
Ctrl-u clears the whole command line
Up finds older matching entries
Down goes back toward newer ones
In : or /, Ctrl-p goes to older history
PageUp / PageDown do the same
:history shows : command history
Use :history / for search history
Type :N then press Enter
Example: :120 jumps to line 120
Lists stored jump locations
Pair with Ctrl-o / Ctrl-i
:marks shows active marks
Use it before jumping with ' or `

tips.summary: Insert register in command line Ctrl-r
details: In : or /, Ctrl-r inserts a register
Great for reusing text in :s

tips.summary: Insert word in command line Ctrl-r Ctrl-w
details: In : or /, Ctrl-r Ctrl-w inserts the word under cursor
Useful for reusing names in a command

tips.summary: Insert file in command line Ctrl-r Ctrl-f
details: In : or /, Ctrl-r Ctrl-f inserts the file under cursor
Useful for file-based Ex commands
:help {topic} opens docs (e.g., :help text-objects)
Use Ctrl-] to jump tags, Ctrl-t to jump back
/pattern searches forward
?pattern searches backward
Press Enter to search, Esc to cancel
n goes to the next match
N goes to the previous match
Works after /, ?, *, and #
* searches forward for the exact word
# searches backward for the exact word
* finds the next whole-word match
cw changes it; n . repeats on the next match
Like * and # but allows partial matches
Useful for camelCase and snake_case
:noh (or :nohlsearch) clears highlighting
The current search pattern still stays active
/foo\c makes this search case-insensitive
/Foo\C forces this search to be case-sensitive
/\<word\> matches whole words only
Use it to avoid partial-token matches
After a search, use nzz to jump and recenter
Use Nzz to go back and recenter too

tips.summary: Delete/change up to a search match
details: d/pat<Enter> deletes up to the next match
c?pat<Enter> changes back to the previous match
/pat/e lands on the end of the match
/pat/+2 lands two lines after the match
\V makes most characters literal
Good for paths, JSON, and punctuation

tips.summary: Use \v to reduce escaping in regex
details: \v makes most characters magic
Great for :s patterns with groups
After searching /pat, use :%s//new/g
The empty pattern reuses the last search
Use # or | when the pattern contains /
Example: :%s#old/path#new/path#g
Use :%s/old/new/gc
y/n/a/q/l controls each match

tips.summary: Repeat last :s on this line with &
details: & repeats the last :s on the current line
Useful for applying the same :s line by line

tips.summary: Use \r to insert a newline in :s
details: In the replacement, \r inserts a newline
Useful for reformatting lists and code
In the replacement, & means the whole match
Example: :%s/foo/&_suffix/g
Capture groups in the pattern, then reuse them
Great for swapping or reordering text

tips.summary: Replace matches one by one with cgn
details: Search first with /, *, or #
cgn changes the next match and enters Insert
. repeats the same edit on later matches
:10,20s/a/b/g changes only those lines
Visual :s starts with the '<,'> range

tips.summary: Replace from here to end with :.,$s
details: :.,$s/foo/bar/g starts at the current line
Useful for partial-file refactors

tips.summary: Run a command on matching lines with :g
details: :g/TODO/p prints matching lines
:g/pat/d deletes matching lines
:v/pat/d deletes lines that do not match
Use it when you want to keep only matching lines
:map lists mappings; mode forms narrow the list
Use :nmap or :imap when a key fails in one mode
Use nmap, imap, or vmap for the target mode
Normal, Insert, and Visual maps should stay separate
nnoremap, inoremap, and vnoremap avoid recursion
Safer when your rhs uses keys you also remap
Set it before mappings: let mapleader = " "
Put your personal shortcuts under <Leader>
<silent> stops a map from echoing on the cmdline
Useful for clean, repeatable shortcuts
:unmap removes a mapping; mode forms target one mode
Use :iunmap or :vunmap when only one mode is affected
nnoremap Q <Nop> makes Q do nothing
Useful for disabling keys you hit by accident
In ~/.ideavimrc: map <Leader>f <Action>(ReformatCode)
Use plain map for <Action>(...), not noremap
:source ~/.ideavimrc reloads your config after edits
Useful when you want to test mappings without restarting the IDE
:split opens another split on the current file
Use it when stacked editing is easier

tips.summary: Open a file in a split :split {file}
details: :split {file} opens that file in a new split
:vsplit {file} does it side by side
:vsplit opens a side-by-side view
Useful for compare-style editing

tips.summary: Split from the keyboard Ctrl-w s / Ctrl-w v
details: Ctrl-w s splits horizontally
Ctrl-w v splits vertically
:close or Ctrl-w c closes this split
Use it when you want to keep other splits
:only keeps the current split
Ctrl-w o does the same from Normal mode

tips.summary: Cycle through splits Ctrl-w w / W
details: Ctrl-w w goes to the next split
Ctrl-w W goes to the previous split

tips.summary: Move to a split Ctrl-w h/j/k/l
details: Use h j k l for left down up right
Arrow keys also work after Ctrl-w

tips.summary: Next and previous tab gt / gT
details: gt goes to the next tab
gT goes to the previous tab

tips.summary: Jump to a tab by number {count}gt
details: 2gt jumps to tab 2, 5gt jumps to tab 5
Useful when you have several tabs open

tips.summary: Next and previous tab with Ex :tabn / :tabp
details: :tabn moves to the next tab
:tabp moves to the previous tab
:tabclose closes the active tab page
Use it when you want to keep the rest
:tabonly closes all other tab pages
Use it to reduce a crowded tab layout
:tabmove 0 moves the tab to the start
:tabmove without a number moves it last
zf{motion} folds a motion like zfip
Works where IDE folding is available
zo opens, zc closes, za toggles
Works where IDE folding is available

tips.summary: Open/close nested folds zO / zC / zA
details: zO opens nested folds, zC closes them
zA toggles the whole fold recursively
zj moves to the next fold start
zk moves to the previous fold start
zd deletes one fold at the cursor
zD deletes nested folds there too

tips.summary: Fold all / unfold all zM / zR
details: zM closes all folds in view
zR opens all folds in view

tips.summary: Control fold depth with zr / zm
details: zr opens one fold level
zm closes one fold level
In :, :action ToggleIdeaVim flips it
Useful when a key conflict gets in the way
In :, :action ReformatCode runs it
Use <Action>(...) in mappings instead
:actionlist extract filters by name
Use it before mapping IDE actions
Use :set trackactionids
IdeaVim shows the action ID in notices

tips.summary: Use IDE smart join with :set ideajoin
details: Then J uses the IDE smart join
Useful when syntax should be adjusted

tips.summary: Keep your mode during rename edits
details: Use :set idearefactormode=keep
Rename edits stop forcing Select mode
A-Z marks sync to IDE bookmarks
Useful for cross-file jumping

tips.summary: Use IDE save hooks with :set ideawrite=all
details: Use :set ideawrite=all
:w behaves like IDE Save All

tips.summary: Let IDE completion keep Tab and Enter
details: Use :set lookupkeys? to inspect them
These keys stay with the IDE popup

tips.summary: Open IdeaVim Tutor from the status bar
details: Click the Vim indicator in the IDE status bar
Choose Tutor from that menu
In Insert, Ctrl-Space opens completion
Press it again to widen suggestions
Use :set paragraph-motion to enable the plugin
{ and } stop treating only fully empty lines as paragraph breaks
Use :set matchit to enable the plugin
% jumps across tags and control blocks, not just brackets

tips.summary: Jump to two-character targets with sneak
details: Use :set sneak to enable the plugin
sfo jumps forward to fo; Sfo searches backward
Use :set highlightedyank to enable the plugin
Yanks briefly flash so you can confirm what was copied

tips.summary: Replace text from a register with ReplaceWithRegister
details: Use :set ReplaceWithRegister to enable the plugin
gr{motion} replaces text with the current register; "agrw uses register a

tips.summary: Comment text with commentary gcc / gc
details: Use :set commentary to enable the plugin
gcc comments a line; gc works with motions and Visual selections

tips.summary: Add, change, or delete surroundings with surround
details: Use :set surround to enable the plugin
ysiw) adds parens; cs"' swaps quote types; ds" removes them
Use :set exchange to enable the plugin
cxiw marks one region; repeat on a second target to swap
Use :set argtextobj to enable the plugin
cia changes one argument; daa deletes one

tips.summary: Select the whole buffer with ae / ie
details: Use :set textobj-entire to enable the plugin
vae or vie selects the whole buffer

tips.summary: Target an indent block with ii / ai
details: Use :set indent-object to enable the plugin
vai selects the current indent block; dii deletes it

tips.summary: Open the project tree with NERDTree
details: Use :set NERDTree to enable the plugin
:NERDTreeToggle opens the tree; :NERDTreeFind reveals the current file
Use :set functiontextobj to enable the plugin
vif selects the inner function; yaf yanks a function
Use :set multiple-cursors to enable the plugin
Map <C-n> to <Plug>NextWholeOccurrence, then press <C-n> to add matches
Use :set switch to enable the plugin
gs flips pairs like true/false and &&/||
